message:
fix(viewer): scope cycle navigator window events to insights view and gate lifecycle on panel visibility

- Add \$watch('view') to cycle navigator that resets active state,
  currentIndex, and currentPath when the view leaves insights, preventing
  stale cycle highlight state from persisting across view switches
- Gate bv-graph:cycle-highlight-change and cycle-navigator-reset window
  event handlers on view === 'insights', preventing hidden-view state
  from being updated by graph events that fire in the background
- Add viewer_runtime_scopes_cycle_navigator_window_events_to_insights_view
  test asserting the view watch, highlight guard, and reset guard
- Sync beads issue tracker and history snapshots

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -211,6 +211,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-4h6","title":"Cache layer: hash-based invalidation with TTL for graph metrics","description":"WHAT: Add a computation cache that stores previously computed graph metrics keyed by a hash of the input graph structure, with configurable TTL expiry. WHY: Go caches analysis results (pkg/analysis/cache.go) so repeated triage/plan/insights calls on unchanged data are near-instant. Our Rust engine recomputes everything from scratch every time. For interactive TUI use, this causes noticeable lag when switching between views. IMPLEMENTATION: Create src/analysis/cache.rs with GraphMetricsCache struct. Key = SHA-256 of sorted (node_ids + edge_pairs). Value = all computed metrics (PageRank scores, betweenness, HITS, etc.). TTL configurable via AnalysisConfig (default 5 minutes). Cache is in-memory only (no disk persistence needed). Wire into GraphAnalyzer so compute_all_metrics checks cache first. ACCEPTANCE: Second call with same graph returns cached results in <1ms. Cache invalidates when graph changes. TTL expiry works. Cache hit/miss stats available for debugging. PARENT: bd-3in","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:02:49.091260977Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:23:35.423439122Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T02:23:35.420164320Z","close_reason":"Created analysis/cache.rs with MetricsCache: SHA-256 keyed in-memory cache with configurable TTL (default 5 min), automatic expired-entry eviction, hit/miss stats. 9 unit tests (hit, miss, status-change invalidation, TTL expiry, clear, deterministic key, config-sensitive key, empty graph, default impl). All 725 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":43,"issue_id":"bd-4h6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN: Use a simple HashMap<[u8; 32], CachedMetrics> where the key is SHA-256 of the graph structure. CachedMetrics holds all computed values plus a timestamp. On cache check: hash current graph, look up, check TTL. Thread-safe via Arc<RwLock<>>. No disk persistence — cache is session-scoped. Memory bound: cap at ~100 entries (each is small, just numbers).","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:09:17Z"},{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-4h6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CORRECTION: Previous description said 'No disk persistence needed.' However, Go cache.go has BOTH in-memory and disk caching (robotAnalysisDiskCacheFileName = 'analysis_cache.json'). For full parity we should implement disk caching too, at minimum for robot outputs. Memory cache uses sync.RWMutex (Rust: Arc<RwLock<>>). Disk cache writes to .beads/analysis_cache.json. Also noted: Go uses a global package-level cache instance (globalCache) — Rust should use a similar static or passed-through cache reference.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:33:31Z"},{"id":96,"issue_id":"bd-4h6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING REQUIREMENT: This task is NOT complete until it includes inline unit tests. Config: test load/merge/default. Cache: test hit/miss/TTL/invalidation. Feedback: test round-trip/apply/corrupt-file. Async: test fast-return/eventual-complete.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:37:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-54fy","title":"Support multi-repo workspace baselines","description":"Currently, --save-baseline and --robot-drift only work for single-repo workspaces. Multi-repo workspaces silently skip baseline operations (src/main.rs:2827-2828).

CURRENT BEHAVIOR:
When a .bv/workspace.yaml has multiple enabled repos, the baseline code skips namespacing and saves/loads a flat baseline. This means:
- Baselines saved from multi-repo workspaces may have ID collisions
- Drift detection against such baselines could produce incorrect results
- No error or warning is emitted — it silently operates on un-namespaced data

WHY THIS IS HARD:
Per-repo baselines require:
1. Splitting the baseline file into per-repo sections (or using a directory of baseline files)
2. Namespacing issue IDs consistently between save and load
3. Handling the case where repos are added/removed between baseline snapshots
4. Deciding whether cross-repo drift (e.g., new dependency edge between repos) should be tracked

APPROACH:
1. Save baselines as a JSON object keyed by repo prefix
2. On load, match each section to the current workspace repos
3. Report removed/added repos as drift events
4. Namespace IDs using the same logic as loader::namespace_workspace_issues()

WORKAROUND: Users with multi-repo workspaces can save baselines per-repo by running bvr with --beads-file pointing to each repo individually.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:00.870871066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:59:36.565920481Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:59:36.565121849Z","close_reason":"Added multi-repo workspace warnings for --save-baseline, --check-drift, --robot-drift. Full per-repo baseline support deferred as future feature (commit 8f2856f).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","drift","workspace"]}
